name: Chest-Supported Row
resistance_profile: Even tension through the row; chest support removes lower-back involvement so you can train the back hard near failure.
rom_notes: Allow the shoulder blades to protract and arms to extend fully at the front, then retract and row to the torso.
cues: Let the shoulder blades stretch forward at the front
Row with the elbows, squeeze the back
Keep the chest on the pad (no torso english)
execution_steps: Set the incline pad so your chest rests on it with your arms hanging down.
Grip the handles or dumbbells and let the shoulder blades stretch forward.
Row by driving the elbows back, squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Pause at the contraction, then lower to full arm extension, keeping your chest on the pad.
good_when: You want to train the back hard near failure without lower-back fatigue.
You tend to heave or use torso english on free-weight rows and want strict form.
bad_when: No bench or machine is available.
You specifically want to train the hinge and lower-back stabilization (use a barbell row).
common_errors: Short range at the contracted end only
